commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes memory-core fallback vector search to scan chunks in 256-row rowid batches with `setImmediate` yields, updates regression tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and supplied live output. Current main synchronously scans fallback vector rows when sqlite-vec is unavailable, and the PR body shows the before/after heartbeat behavior through the actual `searchVector` fallback path.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed; the opted-in automerge path can rely on exact-head checks and mergeability gates.

target.title: fix(cli): preserve numeric config set record keys
target.body:
Fixes #83331.

Behavior or issue addressed:
- `openclaw config set channels.discord.guilds.<snowflake>.requireMention true` could create `guilds` as an array because the next path segment was all digits.
- Discord guild IDs are numeric-looking strings and `channels.discord.guilds` is a record/object field, so the path writer must keep those segments as object keys.
- This preserves the existing numeric-index behavior generally, but opts known Discord record parent paths out of the missing-parent array heuristic.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: `config set` now preserves a numeric-looking Discord guild ID as an object key under `channels.discord.guilds` instead of materializing `guilds` as an array.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS using the real config CLI command path through `runConfigCommand`, including path parsing, mutation, schema validation, and write preparation. File writing is captured by the repo test harness so the local user config is not modified.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```console
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/config-cli.test.ts -t "keeps numeric config set path segments"
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/andy/openclaw-83331

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 95 skipped (96)
```

Observed result after fix: The command path equivalent to `openclaw config set channels.discord.guilds.1495587801394184362.requireMention true --strict-json` writes this object shape and confirms the parent is not an array:
```json
{
  "channels": {
    "discord": {
      "guilds": {
        "1495587801394184362": {
          "requireMention": true
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

What was not tested: A native installed `openclaw` binary writing a real user config file. The repo CLI command path was tested in-process with mocked writes to avoid touching local config.

## Validation
Full validation:
```console
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/config-cli.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  96 passed (96)
```

Other checks:
```console
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/config-cli.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/cli/config-cli.ts
git diff --check
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Commit author verification:
```console
6fb04b8643 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> fix(cli): preserve numeric config set record keys
```
